item.title: India RBI Paid Record Dividend From Currency Gains, Report Says
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India paid a record dividend to the government after higher interest income and large gains from foreign exchange transactions boosted earnings, the central bank’s annual report showed.

item.title: ECB’s Panetta States Case for Rate Hike Without Pre-Set Path
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta acknowledged the case for an interest-rate hike while urging colleagues not to flag subsequent tightening.

item.title: Italy Is Ripe for a New Phase of Banking M&A Deals, Panetta Says
item.description: Bank of Italy Governor Fabio Panetta said the country’s robust finance industry has scope for a new phase of dealmaking, both domestically and within the region.

item.title: Taiwan Lifts 2026 Growth Outlook to More Than 9% on AI Hunger
item.description: Taiwan revised up its economic growth forecast for this year as demand for tech goods in the AI era tops worries about the Iran war boosting energy costs.

item.title: Fuel Prices Push Kenya Inflation Sharply Higher on War Shock
item.description: Kenyan inflation accelerated to a more than two-year high in May, driven by a sharp Iran-war induced increase in local fuel prices.

item.title: Gita Gopinath on Why Interest Rates Have Surged All Around the World
item.description: The growing signs that governments are running out of fiscal room.

item.title: Odd Lots: Gopinath on Worldwide Surging Interest Rates (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Gopinath on Worldwide Surging Interest Rates (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Gopinath on Worldwide Surging Interest Rates (Podcast)】

item.title: AI is Creating More Work for Australia’s Workplace Tribunal
item.description: Australia’s Fair Work Commission announced a review of its processes to cope with an influx of cases being lodged with the help of generative AI assistance tools, which it said has contributed to an estimated 70% workload increase over a three-year period.

item.title: Polish Inflation Unexpectedly Slows, Cooling Rate-Hike Risks
item.description: Polish inflation unexpectedly decelerated, easing pressure on policymakers to quickly adjust policy to the global spike in prices.

item.title: Indian Firms Face Tougher Investor Scrutiny as Rupee Hits Record Lows
item.description: The rupee’s relentless slide is prompting tougher scrutiny of Indian companies as investors and analysts press executives on their foreign-exchange strategy.

item.title: Fed’s Kashkari Says It’s Too Early to Call Time for Rate Hikes
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari said it’s too early to conclude that interest rates need to rise, while arguing that the central bank should keep all options on the table.

item.title: China’s Wuxi Taclink Is Said to Mull Rare Listing in Singapore
item.description: Wuxi Taclink Optoelectronics Technology Co. is considering a listing in Singapore, a potential first for any company that already trades shares in mainland China, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Indian Power Firms Snap Up Gas to Meet Night-Time Cooling Demand
item.description: India’s power generation companies have quadrupled their natural gas purchases from a domestic bourse over the past couple of months to meet surging power demand from searing summer heat.

item.title: HSG Is Said to Be in Lead to Buy Blackstone-Backed Leica Camera
item.description: HSG has emerged as the leading bidder for a stake in German high-end camera maker Leica Camera AG, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: $2.3 Trillion Norway Fund Backs Human Rights Review at Palantir
item.description: Norway’s $2.3 trillion wealth fund supported all shareholder proposals at Palantir Technologies Inc. as the fund’s growing politicization puts its investments under closer scrutiny by the Nordic country’s public.

item.title: US Bitcoin ETFs Bleed $2.8 Billion in Longest Outflow Streak
item.description: Investors pulled money from US spot-Bitcoin exchange-traded funds for a ninth straight session, the longest run of withdrawals since the products debuted, underscoring a cooling in demand for the largest cryptocurrency even as broader risk assets rally.

item.title: Ocado Reaches Deal to Upgrade Asda’s Online Sales Systems
item.description: Ocado Group Plc struck a deal to help British grocer Asda lift online sales, a boost for the company’s automated technology after a series of setbacks.

item.title: Euro-Zone Consumer Price Expectations May Rise More, ECB Warns
item.description: The Iran war may push medium-term inflation expectations among euro-area consumers higher, according to a European Central Bank blog post that appears to underpin the case for hiking interest rates.

item.title: French and Spanish Inflation Jumps, Boosting Case for ECB Hike
item.description: Inflation in France and Spain accelerated to the fastest level since 2024, backing the case for the European Central Bank to raise interest rates.

item.title: Finland’s Economy Grows at Fastest Pace in Almost Five Years
item.description: Finland’s economy clocked in broad-based growth in the first quarter, providing some relief to a country that has struggled with rising public debt after years of low growth.

item.title: Japan Turns to Anime to Help Kids Avoid Bears As Attacks Triple
item.description: Struggling to rein in a surge in bear attacks, Japan has turned to anime to teach children how to avoid a potentially deadly encounter.

item.title: Indonesia’s Surprisingly Good GDP Has Analysts Doubting the Data
item.description: Economists are increasingly doubting Indonesia’s robust growth narrative under President Prabowo Subianto, with the skepticism spilling over into a rare public debate over the reliability of the country’s economic data.

item.title: BOJ Will Probably Raise Rates in June, Former Board Member Says
item.description: The Bank of Japan will probably increase its benchmark rate next month at a meeting that will determine whether authorities can keep from falling behind the curve in fighting inflation, according to a former board member.

item.title: Sweden’s Faltering Economy Bolsters Riksbank Rate Hold Plan
item.description: Sweden’s economy shrank for the first time in a year as analysts expected, likely reassuring the nation’s central bank that it can wait before raising interest rates.

item.title: Taiwan Brokerages Seek $1.4 Billion of Loans, Adding to Rush
item.description: Three Taiwanese securities firms are seeking around $1.4 billion of new loans, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a rush of recent fundraising as a stock market boom stokes demand for financial services.

item.title: New Buyers Enter Market for Anti-Drone Systems, Terma CEO Says
item.description: When Terma A/S began selling technology to protect critical infrastructure more than a decade ago, its clients were mostly airports, ports, and power facilities in the Middle East. Today, Denmark’s largest defense company is fielding inquiries from embassies and even museums as demand for counter-drone systems surges.

item.title: Stock Futures Steady, Pound Slips on Tentative Ceasefire Extension
Malaysia’s Largest Pharma Chain Big Caring Is Said to Seek $750 Million in IPO
item.description: Big Caring Group Bhd., the largest pharmacy chain in Malaysia, is looking to raise as much as 3 billion ringgit ($750 million) in its initial public offering, potentially one of the country’s biggest listings in the past decade, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Alpac Capital Agrees to Buy Adria News Network From United Group
item.description: Alpac Capital agreed to buy Adria News Network, which has news assets in Bosnia, Croatia, Montenegro, Serbia and Slovenia, from United Group BV, according to a statement released Friday.

item.title: Fried Chicken Cutlet Chain Zhengxin Food Is Said to Consider Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Shanghai Zhengxin Food Group Co., a chain specializing in fried chicken cutlets, is exploring an initial public offering in Hong Kong that may raise about $300 million, people with knowledge of the matter said.
